page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t6-c1m4.large is a burstable, budget-oriented virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 2 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the lower tiers of compute and memory capabilities. In stress-ng testing, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance fall into the bottom 25% tier. Memory bandwidth tests, including read-only and write operations on large blocks, similarly rank in the bottom 25%. Passmark CPU and memory scores are low, with database operations and encryption tests scoring in the bottom 10%. Additionally, static web serving throughput and Redis SET operations register in the bottom 10% and bottom 25% respectively.

Given its burstable CPU allocation and lack of local storage, the ecs.t6-c1m4.large is designed for low-intensity workloads that experience occasional spikes in demand. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for development environments, low-traffic web servers, code repositories, and light background tasks. However, it is not suitable for sustained high-performance computing, database hosting, or memory-intensive applications due to its low baseline performance and resource limitations.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t6-c1m4.large is a burstable virtual machine instance designed for low-intensity workloads. Running on the KVM hypervisor, it is equipped with an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor operating at 2.5 GHz, providing 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM. The instance features no local storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. Performance benchmarks consistently place this server in the bottom 25% for CPU and memory bandwidth, with database operations and encryption testing falling into the bottom 10%. Due to its burstable CPU allocation, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for non-production environments, light background processing, and low-traffic web serving, but it is poorly suited for sustained compute-heavy or data-intensive applications.
